---
title: "cfDNApipe Data Analysis"
author: "Trish"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r loading datafiles and packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(table1)
library(knitr)

cfDNApipeData <- read_excel("C:/Users/pquin/OneDrive - National University of Ireland, Galway/Research Project/R_Code/cfDNApipeData.xlsx")

```
```{r tables and graphs from data}
cfDNApipeData %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(sample.size = n(),
            mean.peak1 = round(mean(Peak_1), 1),
            SD.peak1 = round(sd(Peak_1), 1),
            mean.peak2 = round(mean(Peak_2), 1),
            SD.peak2 = round(sd(Peak_2), 1)
  )

#using table1 package for formatted table
table1(~ Peak_1 + Peak_2 | Group, data=cfDNApipeData) 

saveRDS(table1, "cfDNApipeLengths.rds")

kable(table1,
      format = "latex",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      caption = "A summary of cfDNA lengths for cancer and control treatments")

#graph comparing peak 1 for both groups
cfDNApipeData %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Peak_1, y = Group, fill = Group)) + 
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, colour = "yellow", geom = "point", shape =18, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot comparing Peak 1 values for the Cancer and Normal groups", 
       subtitle = "(mean symbol superimposed)" ) +
  xlab("Fragment Size (Base Pairs)") +
  ylab("") + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")

#graph comparing peak 2 for both groups
cfDNApipeData %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Peak_2, y = Group, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, colour = "yellow", geom = "point", shape = 18, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot comparing Peak 2 values for the Cancer and Normal groups",
       subtitle = "(mean symbol superimposed)") +
  xlab("Fragment Size (Base Pairs)") +
  ylab("") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

#Welch's t-test for peak 1 in both groups
t.test(Peak_1 ~ Group, conf.level = 0.95,
       data = cfDNApipeData)

#Welch's t-test for peak 2 in both groups
t.test(Peak_2 ~ Group, conf.level = 0.95,
       data = cfDNApipeData)

#Wilcox test for peak 1 in both groups
wilcox.test(Peak_1 ~ Group, conf.int = TRUE, 
            conf.level = 0.95, data = cfDNApipeData)
  

#Wilcox test for peak 2 in both groups
wilcox.test(Peak_2 ~ Group, conf.int = TRUE, 
            conf.level = 0.95, data = cfDNApipeData)

#plotting peak fragment lenghths

stripchart(cfDNApipeData$Peak_1)

stripchart(cfDNApipeData$Peak_2)

boxplot(cfDNApipeData$Peak_1, horizontal = TRUE)


```
